description: Execute a plan until it is complete

steps.label: Planner Select And Store Next Plan

content: Use fresh disk reads and current git state, not conversational memory. Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` from disk first and use only the stored `plan_path` and `additional_repositories` as the active scope for this flow. Re-open the exact relative `plan_path` from disk even if you have read it before. Verify that the selected plan file exists in the current repository and that the story number in the current repository branch name matches the story number in the selected plan filename. Re-check the current repository branch directly from git, for example with `git branch --show-current`, and re-check the most recent commit directly from git, for example with `git log --oneline -1`. If either of those scope checks fails, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. Then verify that every additional repository still exists, is readable, and is still on a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename. Re-check each additional repository branch directly from git, for example with `git -C <repo_root> branch --show-current`, and re-check its most recent commit directly from git, for example with `git -C <repo_root> log --oneline -1`. If an additional repository path is missing, invalid, or unreadable, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. If an additional repository no longer has a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename, stop and say repository branch scope has drifted and must be repaired before continuing. You MUST double-check to see if tasks have been added to the plan by another agent or if anything has changed. You must ensure you fully understand the plan and its remaining tasks before proceeding. You MUST read the whole plan again and CANNOT assume you understand it from before. Once you have done this, give me a summary of the remaining work.

steps.label: Task Execution And Blocker Repair Loop
Check for blocker before automated proof
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` first and re-open the exact relative `plan_path` from disk before answering. Scan the plan from bottom to top and identify the highest-numbered task whose `Task Status` is either `__in_progress__` or `__done__`. Does that task currently contain a `**BLOCKER**` note in its Implementation Notes? Answer only yes or no as JSON: {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Run automated proof and fix issues
Check for blocker before manual testing
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` first and re-open the exact relative `plan_path` from disk before answering. Scan the plan from bottom to top and identify the highest-numbered task whose `Task Status` is either `__in_progress__` or `__done__`. Does that task currently contain a `**BLOCKER**` note in its Implementation Notes? Answer only yes or no as JSON: {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Check if manual testing added follow-up work
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` first and re-open the exact relative `plan_path` from disk before answering. Scan the plan from bottom to top and identify the highest-numbered task whose `Task Status` is either `__done__` or `__in_progress__`. Did your latest manual testing run add any new unchecked subtasks or new unchecked testing steps to that task? Answer only yes or no as JSON: {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Research blocker solution and prove it
Refresh current plan handoff after planner edits

content: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` first and re-open the exact relative `plan_path` stored there after the planning agent step. Read the end of the plan file from disk and report the highest `### <number>.` task heading currently present. Verify that the selected plan file still exists in the current repository and that the story number in the current repository branch name matches the story number in the selected plan filename. If either of those checks fails, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. Then verify that every additional repository still exists, is readable, and is still on a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename. If an additional repository path is missing, invalid, or unreadable, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. If an additional repository no longer has a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename, stop and say repository branch scope has drifted and must be repaired before continuing. Confirm any Task Status updates or new tasks that were added, or any task renumbering that occurred, and run `git status -sb` to verify the plan file change was committed. If you conclude there is no next task, you must explicitly confirm that the highest-numbered task is `__done__` and that no later task heading exists. Then, identify the next executable task based on the updated plan, not based on what used to be blocked, and summarize the remaining tasks based on the updated plan before proceeding.

Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` first and open only the relative `plan_path` stored there in the current repository. Do not rediscover the story independently. Before answering completion, list each task's Task Status (including the final task). Explicitly state whether the final task is __done__ or not, cite the relative plan file path you read, and state whether every repository in scope is still on a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename or whether repository branch scope has drifted.

steps.label: Check for completion
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` first and use only the stored `plan_path` and `additional_repositories` as the active scope for this flow. Verify that the selected plan file exists in the current repository and that the story number in the current repository branch name matches the story number in the selected plan filename. If either of those checks fails, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. Then verify that every additional repository still exists, is readable, and is still on a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename. If an additional repository path is missing, invalid, or unreadable, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. If an additional repository no longer has a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename, stop and say repository branch scope has drifted and must be repaired before continuing. Then re-read that plan and check the last 3 git commits in the current repository and in every additional repository. You MUST confirm the final task's Task Status from the plan file and only answer yes if every task is marked __done__ and every repository in scope is still on a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename. Is the plan that was being worked on now complete? You MUST re-read the plan and check the last 3 git commits before giving your answer as the plan probably has changes made by other agents since you last looked at it. You must give only a yes or no answer. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Refresh current plan handoff after review
Check for completion
steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` first and use only the stored `plan_path` and `additional_repositories` as the active scope for this flow. Verify that the selected plan file exists in the current repository and that the story number in the current repository branch name matches the story number in the selected plan filename. If either of those checks fails, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. Then verify that every additional repository still exists, is readable, and is still on a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename. If an additional repository path is missing, invalid, or unreadable, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. If an additional repository no longer has a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename, stop and say repository branch scope has drifted and must be repaired before continuing. Then re-read that plan and check the last 3 git commits in the current repository and in every additional repository. Is the plan that was being worked on now complete? You MUST re-read that plan and check the last 3 git commits before giving your answer, as the plan probably has changes made by other agents since you last looked at it. You must give only a yes or no answer. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.
